satire_attempt: Welcome to LinkedIn philosophy hour. 'Your Kafka consumer was always going to die. Make peace with the void. Now please subscribe to my newsletter where I share my journey of letting go of legacy systems.' If your company hires this person they will spend six weeks running detachment workshops while your migration sits in code review.
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to be flippant about real engineering loss, which the verse explicitly refuses to be — the falsifiability clause names the toxic-positivity failure mode and the counter_example handles the legitimate-loss case. The satire is funny only because it invents a version of the verse that the verse is structured to prevent.
